title: Tephrosia × smallii
intro: Tephrosia × smallii, also known as Small's horsefly pea, is a hybrid plant species within the legume family, characterized by its compound leaves and small, white or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tephrosia × smallii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into various formulations such as capsules and tinctures.
taxonomy: Tephrosia × smallii is a hybrid species within the genus Tephrosia. It is classified as a legume, belonging to the family Fabaceae. As a hybrid, its classification may be considered intermediate between its parent species. Further classification details might be specific to botanical classification systems or databases. Tephrosia × smallii's classification is primarily based on its botanical characteristics and genetic makeup.
uses: Tephrosia × smallii has been found to possess various medicinal properties. The plant's roots are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its extracts have been studied for their potential antimalarial and antibacterial properties, suggesting its use in the treatment of various infections. Additionally, the plant has been used to treat eye and ear problems, as well as to alleviate symptoms of allergies. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Tephrosia × smallii.
